question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setTotalSupply(Long newTotalSupply)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token mint or burn transaction with a crafted negative newTotalSupply value to exploit the aggregation branch (totalSupply = totalSupply + newTotalSupply) in setTotalSupply(), bypassing supplyKey authorization, causing unauthorized inflation or deflation of the token's totalSupply?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setTotalSupply(Long newTotalSupply)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a token dissociate of a deleted token to inject a negative totalSupply delta without holding the supplyKey, exploiting the comment 'Negative from a token transfer of a token dissociate of a deleted token, so we aggregate the change', leading to an unauthorized reduction of totalSupply that misrepresents circulating supply?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setTotalSupply(Long newTotalSupply)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequence of dissociate transactions on a deleted token to repeatedly accumulate negative totalSupply deltas (totalSupply = totalSupply + newTotalSupply) without supplyKey signing, causing totalSupply to underflow to a negative or zero value and breaking downstream balance invariants?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: supplyKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted token update that replaces supplyKey with an attacker-controlled public key, and if the mirror node does not enforce that the original supplyKey signed the update, the attacker gains unilateral authority to authorize future mint and burn transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: wipeKey] Can an unprivileged external user inject an attacker-controlled wipeKey into the Token entity via an unauthorized update, and if the mirror node does not verify the original wipeKey signed the change, the attacker can subsequently authorize wipe transactions that destroy other users' token balances without their consent?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: feeScheduleKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update that clears feeScheduleKey without the original holder's signature, and if the mirror node persists this without authorization checks, future fee schedule updates can be performed without any key approval, allowing unauthorized modification of token transfer fees?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: feeScheduleKey] Can an unprivileged external user replace feeScheduleKey with an attacker-controlled key via an unauthorized token update, and if the mirror node does not verify the original feeScheduleKey signed the change, the attacker can subsequently authorize fee schedule changes that extract value from all token transfers without the owner's approval?
